title: ulmus angustifolia
intro: Ulmus angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved elm, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its distinctive narrow leaves and small flowers. The bark and leaves of this plant contain tannins, which possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ulmus angustifolia include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of various skin conditions. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
